div.p: Thread A stalls mid-enqueue → Thread B detects lagging tail → Thread B helps complete
Key Insight: The helping mechanism ensures lock-free progress.

ul.li: Thread A's tail swing is best-effort
If A stalls, any other thread can complete it
The system progresses even if individual threads block
This is why MS queue is truly lock-free, not just wait-free

g.text: 11. No problem! B already did it
